markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games’ second delay of Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19, 2026 has extended the window for high-profile events to resolve ahead of launch, shaping trader consensus on the multi-outcome Polymarket. The additional months for polish, confirmed in Take-Two’s November 2025 earnings update, have boosted implied probabilities for outcomes such as a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire and major music releases like Drake’s Iceman album. Industry watchers note how the extended timeline aligns with typical campaign cycles for both geopolitics and entertainment, where streaming milestones, chart debuts, and diplomatic developments can accelerate quickly. Upcoming catalysts include Take-Two’s next earnings call and any new GTA VI trailer drops, which could further influence momentum if they signal stable production. The market’s skin-in-the-game pricing captures real capital betting on which listed milestone crosses the finish line first amid an unpredictable cultural and news cycle.

Historical patterns of Rockstar prioritizing quality over rushed schedules further reinforce market-implied odds above 99 percent. markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials and ongoing appeals in Harvey Weinstein’s New York cases have anchored trader sentiment around no additional prison time, with the 75.9% implied probability reflecting his age, health concerns, and more than six years already served since the original 2020 conviction. The latest New York retrial ended in a hung jury on May 15, 2026, following a mixed 2025 verdict that produced only one conviction on a criminal sexual act count. Weinstein’s separate 16-year California sentence from 2022 remains under appeal, with courts historically scrutinizing procedural issues in these high-profile proceedings. Upcoming appellate rulings and any potential sentencing hearings could still shift momentum, though the current market consensus treats further incarceration as increasingly unlikely given the procedural setbacks and time already credited.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions the Carolina Hurricanes as slight favorites to win the 2026 Stanley Cup at 56.5% implied probability, ahead of the Vegas Golden Knights at 42.4% and the Montreal Canadiens at 1.5%. markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering of implied probabilities among Spain, France, England, and Portugal reflects the deep talent pools and strong recent form across Europe’s elite sides heading into 2026 World Cup qualifying and the expanded 48-team tournament. markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s entrenched position as General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party and Chairman of the Central Military Commission underpins the 92.8% trader consensus against removal before the end of 2026. Ongoing personnel adjustments through anti-corruption campaigns, including recent military leadership changes, have further centralized authority without visible elite opposition or designated successor. High-level diplomatic engagements in 2026, such as bilateral and multilateral meetings spanning neighboring regions, Europe, North America, and the Global South, along with his New Year address projecting continuity on issues like Taiwan reunification, signal stability rather than transition pressures. Analysts note the absence of confirmed public health concerns or institutional challenges ahead of the 21st Party Congress scheduled for late 2027, where any leadership adjustments would likely preserve his role within the market’s resolution window.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential field has produced a fragmented market where Gavin Newsom holds the largest share of trader consensus at 23.9 percent, reflecting his profile as a sitting governor of a major state with extensive national fundraising networks and executive experience. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Kamala Harris follow at 9.2 percent and 8.2 percent respectively, driven by distinct bases among progressive activists and prior high-visibility national roles. Remaining contenders such as Jon Ossoff, Josh Shapiro, and Pete Buttigieg trade in the low-to-mid single digits, underscoring how early positioning hinges on factors including state-level performance, Senate or gubernatorial records, and coalition-building potential ahead of primaries. No candidate exceeds 24 percent, illustrating how support could consolidate through 2026 midterm results, polling trends among key voting blocs, or formal endorsements that shift implied probabilities.
